untranslatable_reason: Twin Peaks pattern detection requires multi-bar pattern recognition (identification of two peaks and the confirmation line between them) which cannot be expressed using single-bar indicator comparisons. The signal requires: (1) detection of two distinct peaks in historical price data, (2) identification of the lowest low between these peaks, (3) measurement from the date of the highest high at the right peak to breakout date. This requires custom pattern detection logic not available in the standard indicator set.
translation_notes: This signal describes a classic candlestick/price action pattern (Twin Peaks) that requires custom pattern recognition. The entry condition 'price closes below confirmation line (lowest low between peaks)' and the measurement methodology cannot be reliably translated to indicator-based rules without pattern detection capabilities. A backtest engine would need dedicated Twin Peaks detection logic or this rule should be implemented as a custom pattern scanner outside the standard indicator framework.
